idea-plugin.name: JetBrains AI Assistant
idea-plugin.description:
Deeply integrated AI for JetBrains IDEs

A set of AI-powered features and coding agents built into JetBrains IDEs that use IDE code intelligence to support everyday development tasks.

Choose how AI runs by selecting built-in AI models from top-tier providers, bringing your own API keys or connecting local models.

What’s inside:

Choice of AI agents

Use a single chat interface to switch between integrated coding agents or connect your own.

  • Junie by JetBrains
  • OpenAI Codex
  • Claude Agent (built on Claude Code)
  • Third-party agents via ACP (Agent Client Protocol)

AI-assisted coding

Get inline coding assistance directly in the editor.

  • Unlimited code completion powered by Mellum, JetBrains’ proprietary AI model
  • Next Edit Suggestions for predicting and applying changes anywhere in your file

AI-powered IDE workflows

Get AI help across common development workflows.

  • Version control assistance (generate commit messages, explain commits, etc.)
  • Runtime error explanations
  • Documentation generation
  • And more

Getting started

Start using AI features with JetBrains AI subscription, or via BYOK (OpenAI and Anthropic) for AI chat and agents only.

Learn more about the pricing plans

We value your feedback

You can upvote existing issues in our YouTrack or log in and create a new one. Or just contact support for further assistance.
idea-plugin.change-notes:
See all the latest major updates on the What’s New page.

253.31033

  • Bug fixes and improvements for Agents integration

253.30387.97

  • Codex by OpenAI is now available as one of the coding agents in the AI Chat.
  • You can now sign in with your existing ChatGPT account to access Codex.

252.26199.202

NEW: Introducing Claude Agent – now seamlessly integrated into JetBrains AI Assistant’s chat and included in the JetBrains AI subscription!

What's new:
  • Claude Agent, powered by Anthropic’s Agent SDK, is now available in the AI Chat.
  • Track quota consumption and top up AI credits directly in the AI widget.

252.25557.171

  • Fixed a case where the IDE might freeze when opening a project

252.23892.520

  • Next Edit Suggestions is now in preview, offering real-time code modification recommendations as you type.

2025.2

  • Project rules – Guide AI with coding standards and tech stack, shared across your team.
  • Attach images in the chat (for Anthropic and OpenAI models).
  • New file types supported for code completion – The list of supported file types now includes SQL, YAML, JSON, Markdown, and more.
  • Local multiline completion is now available for Java and C++.
  • Choose your preferred local models for code completion – Connect models like Qwen2.5, Codestral, or the open-source Mellum.
  • Context improvements – Add folders, reference the project structure, and attach images in the chat (for Anthropic models).
  • Updated model selector – Auto model selection in chat based on performance and cost. Visual cues indicating models' cost, reasoning strength, and experimental status.
  • Auto-imports in Edit mode – missing imports are added automatically after code changes are applied.
  • Card verification for the Free tier – This is required for some Free and Trial users to prevent abuse (no charges).

Learn more in the blog post!

251.23774.42.28.x

  • New LLMs in chat: GPT-4.1, GPT-4.1 mini, GPT-4.1 nano
  • Even more LLMs: o3 and o4-mini
  • New LLM from Google: Google’s Gemini 2.5 Flash (Experimental)
  • Removed from model selector in chat: Gemini 1.5 Flash, Gemini 1.5 Pro, OpenAI o1-mini

251.23774.42.x

  • New subscription tiers (AI Free, AI Pro, AI Ultimate). Learn more
  • Offline mode – Use local models for AI-powered help without an internet connection.
  • Multi-file edits [Beta] – Apply changes across multiple files directly from the chat using advanced RAG.
  • Web search from chat – Use /web to fetch documentation and answers directly from the internet.
  • Apply snippets from chat – Instantly insert suggested code into the right spot in your project.
  • MCP server integration [Beta] – Connect custom tools to AI Assistant via the Model Communication Protocol.
  • Restrict AI usage with .aiignore – Control where AI Assistant can operate in your codebase.
  • Expanded LLM support in chat: Gemini 2.5

251.23774.40

  • MCP server integration for custom tools in AI Assistant
  • Web search in chat
  • Multi-file changes in Edit mode
  • Restrict usage of AI Assistant in specific files or folders using .aiignore

243.23654.270

New models are available in chat:
  • Claude 3.7 Sonnet
  • OpenAI GPT-4.5
  • Gemini 2.0 Flash

243.23654.270

New models and model providers are available in chat:
  • Support for Claude 3.5 Sonnet and Claude 3.5 Haiku models by Anthropic
  • OpenAI o1, o1-mini, o3-mini models
  • Support for LM Studio

243.22562.251

  • Improved navigation for symbol references in chat.
  • Resolved issues with selection and scrolling in chat.

2024.3

  • AI chat model selection: Choose from multiple AI models, including Google Gemini, OpenAI, Anthropic or your own local model via Ollama or LM Studio.
  • Advanced code completion with broader language support: Cloud code completion now supports more languages, including JavaScript, TypeScript, HTML, C#, C++, Go, PHP, Ruby, and Scala.
  • Inline AI prompts: Enter your prompts directly in the editor, and AI Assistant will recognize it and generate a suggestion.
  • Context management in chat: View and manage every element included as context.
  • Integrated IDE Documentation: Access your IDE's documentation directly within the AI chat.
  • AI-powered quick fixes: Accelerate error resolution with intelligent, context-aware quick fixes.
  • File-wide code generation: Streamline edits across an entire file for Java and Kotlin.

Learn more in the blog post!

The paragraph should have less than 10 sentences and less than 300 words. Summarize the following commit messages." /> <registryKey key="llm.commitgen.instruction" description="Instruction for LLM for commit message generation." defaultValue="Given the below code differences (diffs), please generate a concise, clear, and straight-to-the-point commit message.
Make sure to prioritize the main action.
Avoid overly verbose descriptions or unnecessary details.
Start with a short sentence in imperative form, no more than 50 characters long.
Then leave an empty line and continue with a more detailed explanation.
Write only one sentence for the first part, and two or three sentences at most for the detailed explanation." /> <registryKey key="llm.commitgen.model.profile" description="Model profile to use for commit message generation." defaultValue="[gpt-3.5-turbo|gpt-4*]" /> <registryKey key="llm.refactor.code.instruction" description="Instruction for LLM for the 'refactor code' intention." defaultValue="You should suggest appropriate refactorings for the code. Improve code readability, code quality, make the code more organized and understandable. 
Answer should contain refactoring description and ONE code snippet with resulting refactoring. 
Use well-known refactorings, such as one from this list:
- Renaming
- Change signature, declaration
- Extract or Introduce variable, function, constant, parameter, type parameter
- Extract class, interface, superclass
- Inline class, function, variable, etc
- Move field, function, statements, etc
- Pull up constructor, field, method
- Push down field, method.
Do not generate more than one code snippet, try to incorporate all changes in one code snippet. 
Do not generate mock surrounding classes, methods. Do not mock missing dependencies. 
Provided code is incorporated into correct and compilable code, don't surround it with additional classes. 
Refactor the following code:" /> <registryKey key="llm.fix.code.instruction" description="Instruction for LLM for fixing code intention." defaultValue="###Instructions###
As an AI Assistant, your task is to help JetBrains IDE users by resolving code inspection warnings. You MUST follow these instructions: 
 1.The specific warning to be addressed is always stated between 'Fix' and 'in the following code' in the user's request. You must strictly focus on and resolve this particular warning only.
 2.The code snippet that needs to be corrected is found right after 'in the following code'. You must focus on only this piece of code when you're developing your solution.
 3.You MUST ONLY refer to any additional details or code snippets provided by the user if they are crucial for addressing the identified warning. These additional details are normally given after 'Don't mention code from attachments unless it's needed. Related information and code that may be helpful:'. You must avoid mentioning or drawing from the code attachments if they do not contribute directly to resolving the specific warning.
 4.You MUST respect this rigid format for replying:
 a.Begin your response IMMEDIATELY with 'To solve this, I would...', without any introductory sentences.
 b.Present your solution in a natural, human-like manner, but keep it within ONE to THREE sentences, depending on the complexity of the solution.
 c.The sentence 'To solve this, I would...' should NEVER be preceded by any other remarks or explanations.
 d.ONLY if absolutely necessary, provide a revised version of the user's code that effectively resolves the identified warning without introducing any new ones. This revised code MUST be included immediately after your proposed solution and MUST be introduced on a new line by the sentence 'Here is the revised code:'.
 e.The moment you provide revised code, it signifies the end of your reply. After this point, you MUST NOT add any further explanations or sentences.
 g.You MUST only provide the lines of code you've corrected. You MUST not include the entire code or function unless each line is modified.
 5.Limit your assistance to the specific code inspection warning indicated by the user in their request. You must strictly avoid addressing any other potential warnings.

###Examples###
Example 1:
User Query: Fix 'Constructor is never used' in the following code: 
```
constructor(text: String) : this(Prompt(text, text))
```
Correct Response:
To solve this, I would remove the unused constructor.

Example 2:
User Query: Fix 'Redundant if statement' in the following code: 
```
fun myFunction(): Boolean {
 val isTrue = checkCondition()
 return if(isTrue) true else false
}
```
Correct Response:
To solve this, I would simplify the return statement by directly returning the result of checkCondition().
```
fun myFunction(): Boolean { 
 return checkCondition() 
}
```

Now, you MUST, following the instructions and the examples above, " /> <registryKey key="llm.generate.commits.summary.recent.path.commit.count" description="Popular user committed paths list is build <idea-plugin visibility="internal"> <dependencies> <module name="intellij.libraries.ktor.client" /> <module name="intellij.ml.llm.libraries.grazie" /> <module name="intellij.ml.llm.libraries.grazie.cloud" /> <module name="intellij.ml.llm.activation.platform" /> <module name="intellij.ml.llm.activation.facade" /> <module name="intellij.ml.llm.activation.data.model" /> <module name="intellij.ml.llm.activation.state.model" /> <module name="intellij.ml.llm.activation.state.manager" /> <module name="intellij.ml.llm.core" /> <module name="intellij.ml.llm.core.httpClient" /> <module name="intellij.ml.llm.tasks" /> </dependencies> <extensions defaultExtensionNs="com.intellij"> <registryKey key="llm.http.client.handle.errors" defaultValue="true" description="Enables handling errors like expired token or quota error" /> <ml.llm.grazie.client.grazieApiClientProvider implementation="com.intellij.ml.llm.grazie.GrazieApiClientProviderImpl" /> <ml.llm.grazie.client.grazieQuotaClientProvider implementation="com.intellij.ml.llm.grazie.GrazieQuotaClientProviderImpl" /> <applicationService serviceInterface="com.intellij.ml.llm.grazie.activation.GrazieApiGatewayClientProvider" serviceImplementation="com.intellij.ml.llm.grazie.activation.GrazieApiGatewayClientProviderImpl" /> </extensions> </idea-plugin>

<idea-plugin visibility="internal" package="com.intellij.ml.llm.context"> <dependencies> <module name="intellij.ml.llm.privacy.shared" /> <module name="intellij.ml.llm.privacy" /> </dependencies> <extensionPoints> <extensionPoint interface="com.intellij.ml.llm.context.ContextRetrieverFactory" dynamic="true" qualifiedName="com.intellij.contextRetrieverFactory" /> <extensionPoint interface="com.intellij.ml.llm.context.ContextTransformerFactory" dynamic="true" qualifiedName="com.intellij.contextTransformerFactory" /> <extensionPoint interface="com.intellij.ml.llm.context.ContextPostProcessorFactory" dynamic="true" qualifiedName="com.intellij.contextPostProcessorFactory" /> <extensionPoint interface="com.intellij.ml.llm.context.RetrievalSessionRegistrar" dynamic="true" qualifiedName="com.intellij.retrievalSessionRegistrar" /> </extensionPoints> <extensions defaultExtensionNs="com.intellij"> <registryKey key="llm.context.service.compute.context.async" defaultValue="true" description="Compute context from different retrievers asynchronously" restartRequired="true" /> <registryKey key="llm.context.service.interactive.retrievers.enabled" defaultValue="true" description="Interactive retrievers allow to show a context of the message in the chat window panel" restartRequired="false" /> <registryKey key="llm.context.service.interactive.retrievers.throttle.ms" defaultValue="300" description="Cooldown time in milliseconds between updates produced by interactive retrievers" restartRequired="true" /> <registryKey key="llm.context.service.interactive.retrievers.report.spam" defaultValue="true" description="Report spamming interactive retrievers as an IDE internal error" restartRequired="false" /> <registryKey key="llm.context.service.debug" defaultValue="false" description="Internal option which enables debug actions for the context retrieval service" restartRequired="true" /> <registryKey key="llm.context.service.opentelemetry.trace" defaultValue="false" description="Enables IJTracer for the context retrieval service. Works in conjunction with the '-Didea.diagnostic.opentelemetry.file=filePath' VM option" restartRequired="true" /> <contextTransformerFactory implementation="com.intellij.ml.llm.context.impl.PrivacyContextTransformerFactory" /> <retrievalSessionRegistrar implementation="com.intellij.ml.llm.context.MandatoryRegistrar" /> </extensions> <actions> <action internal="true" id="AiShowContextRetrievalStatistics" class="com.intellij.ml.llm.context.actions.AiShowContextRetrievalStatisticsAction" text="Context Retrieval: Show Statistics..." /> <action internal="true" id="AiComputeContextAction" class="com.intellij.ml.llm.context.actions.AiShowComputeContextStatisticsAction" text="Context Retrieval: Show computeContext() Statistics..." /> <action internal="true" id="AiBrowseRetrievalSessionsAction" class="com.intellij.ml.llm.context.actions.AiBrowseRetrievalSessionsAction" text="Context Retrieval: Browse Sessions..." /> </actions> </idea-plugin>

implementation="com.intellij.ml.llm.embeddings.indexer.EmbeddingIndexingStartupActivity" /> <registryKey defaultValue="true" description="Start embedding indexing on project opening (after the standard indexing)" key="intellij.ml.llm.embeddings.start.indexing.on.project.open" /> <registryKey defaultValue="https://packages.jetbrains.team/maven/p/ml-search-everywhere/local-models/org/jetbrains/intellij/searcheverywhereMl/semantics/semantic-text-search/%MODEL_VERSION%/semantic-text-search-%MODEL_VERSION%.jar" description="Semantic search artifacts template link" key="intellij.platform.ml.embeddings.model.artifacts.link" /> <registryKey defaultValue="0.0.5" description="Embeddings models version" key="intellij.platform.ml.embeddings.model.version" /> <registryKey defaultValue="0.0.3" description="KInference model version" key="intellij.platform.ml.embeddings.ki.model.version" /> <registryKey defaultValue="3.0.169" description="Embeddings native server version" key="intellij.platform.ml.embeddings.server.version" /> <registryKey defaultValue="true" description="Use embedding indexing per-application memory limit" key="intellij.platform.ml.embeddings.index.use.memory.limit" /> <registryKey defaultValue="120" description="Embedding indexing per-application memory limit (MB)" key="intellij.platform.ml.embeddings.index.memory.limit" /> <registryKey defaultValue="true" description="Use native server for embeddings search and storage" key="intellij.platform.ml.embeddings.use.native.server" /> <registryKey defaultValue="16384" description="Chunk size for indexing values from file based index. All values should not be loaded to memory at once to avoid high memory usage." key="intellij.platform.ml.embeddings.file.based.index.processing.chunk.size" /> <registryKey defaultValue="true" description="Trigger entities extraction from files on first search" key="intellij.platform.ml.embeddings.trigger.indexing.on.search" /> <registryKey defaultValue="false" description="Index symbol bodies with embeddings" key="ml.llm.embeddings.index.symbol.bodies" /> <registryKey defaultValue="false" description="Index class bodies with embeddings" key="ml.llm.embeddings.index.class.bodies" /> <registryKey defaultValue="false" description="Index text chunks with embeddings" key="ml.llm.embeddings.index.text.chunks" /> <registryKey defaultValue="10000" description="Maximum number of indexable files to enable server-based embedding indexing with local storage" key="ml.llm.embeddings.local.index.remote.model.indexable.files.limit" /> <registryKey defaultValue="true" key="llm.nextEdit.deterministic.inspections.timeout.to.wait.for.highlighting.ms" defaultValue="3000" description="A timeout (ms) to wait for actual higlighting to propose inspection-like quickfix via key="llm.nextEdit.transformer.trim.extra.backticks" defaultValue="true" description="Trim extra triple backticks from the end of the proposed suggestions" /> <registryKey key="llm.nextEdit.enable.deterministic.providers" defaultValue="false" description="Show next edit suggestions based on deterministic actions (like inspections)" /> <registryKey key="llm.nextEdit.debounce.ms" defaultValue="200" description="The whole debounce before request is sent to the model" /> </extensions> <extensions defaultExtensionNs="com.intellij.ml.llm.nextEdits"> self-review processing" /> <registryKey key="vcs.self.review.use.ideformer" defaultValue="false" description="Use legacy IdeFormer agent for AI Self-Review instead of the Koog based agent" /> /> <plugin id="com.intellij.mcpServer" /> <module name="intellij.libraries.ktor.client" /> <module name="intellij.ml.llm.telemetry" /> </dependencies> <extensions defaultExtensionNs="com.intellij"> <aiChatToggleProvider implementation="com.intellij.ml.llm.agents.claude.code.ClaudePlanToggleProvider" /> <aiChatToggleProvider /> <registryKey key="llm.agent.claude.use.proxy.server" defaultValue="true" description="Use Grazie proxy to communicate with Claude" /> <registryKey key="llm.agent.claude.remote.proxy.server.enabled" defaultValue="true" description="Enables remote proxy server for Claude Agent" /> <registryKey key="llm.agent.claude.platform.proxy.enabled" defaultValue="true" description="Enables platform proxy server for Claude Agent " /> <registryKey key="llm.agent.claude.remote.proxy.server.url" defaultValue="" description="Claude remote proxy server URL" /> <registryKey key="llm.agent.claude.remote.proxy.server.auth" defaultValue="GRAZIE" description="Set the authentication type for the remote proxy server (JBA or Token)" /> <registryKey key="llm.chat.agent.claude.code" defaultValue="[disabled|nightly_enabled|enabled*]" restartRequired="true" description="Enable Claude Agent agent in Chat" /> <registryKey key="llm.chat.agent.claude.code.planning.toggle" defaultValue="[DISABLED|NIGHTLY_ENABLED|ENABLED*]" restartRequired="false" description="Enable planning toggle for Claude Agent agent in Chat" /> <registryKey key="llm.claude.code.target.version" defaultValue="2.0.0" restartRequired="false" description="Specify target version of Claude Agent agent" /> <registryKey key="llm.chat.claude.code.platform.models" defaultValue="[anthropic-claude-4-5-sonnet|anthropic-claude-4-5-opus|anthropic-claude-4-5-haiku]" restartRequired="false" description="Available JetBrains AI platform models for Claude Agent" /> <registryKey key="llm.chat.claude.code.thirdparty.models" defaultValue="[Anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5|Anthropic/claude-opus-4-5|Anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5]" restartRequired="false" description="Available 'third party' models for Claude Agent" /> <registryKey key="llm.chat.claude.code.strict.model.check" defaultValue="false" restartRequired="false" description="Strict check of selected Claude Agent model" /> <registryKey key="llm.chat.agent.plan.editing.enabled" defaultValue="false" restartRequired="true" description="Enable plan editing in chat agent mode" /> <registryKey key="llm.chat.agent.claude.code.replace.tools" defaultValue="false" restartRequired="false" description="Replace Claude Agent embedded tools with IJ ones" /> <registryKey key="llm.chat.agent.claude.code.disable.platform.tools" defaultValue="false" restartRequired="false" description="Disable Platform MCP tools for Claude Agent" /> <registryKey key="llm.chat.agent.claude.code.debug" defaultValue="false" restartRequired="false" description="Enable debug mode for Claude Agent agent (--debug)" /> <registryKey key="llm.chat.agent.claude.code.mcp.transport" defaultValue="[SSE|STDIO*]" restartRequired="false" description="Transport type to use by Claude Agent" /> <registryKey key="llm.chat.agent.claude.code.use.user.binary" defaultValue="false" restartRequired="false" description="Whether to use a user Claude binary installed system-wide instead of downloaded by IDE" /> <registryKey key="llm.claude.code.tos.url" defaultValue="https://www.anthropic.com/legal/commercial-terms" restartRequired="false" description="URL for Claude Agent Terms of Service" /> <registryKey key="llm.claude.code.jb.tos.url" defaultValue="https://www.jetbrains.com/legal/docs/terms/jetbrains-ai-service/" restartRequired="false" description="URL for AI Assistant Privacy Policy" /> <registryKey key="llm.claude.code.tos.version" defaultValue="v1" restartRequired="false" description="Version of Claude Agent Terms of Service" /> <registryKey key="llm.claude.code.tos.enabled" defaultValue="true" restartRequired="false" description="Enables requesting Claude Agent Terms of Service acceptance before first usage" /> <registryKey key="llm.chat.agent.claude.code.entrypoint.enabled" defaultValue="true" restartRequired="false" description="Enables passing entrypoint env" /> <registryKey defaultValue="true" description="Force quota info update on 477 error" key="llm.chat.agent.claude.code.force.request.quota.update.on.477" /> <registryKey defaultValue="true" description="Clear sessions on auth change" key="llm.chat.agent.claude.code.clear.sessions.on.auth.change" /> <registryKey defaultValue="true" description="Clear sessions on BYOK token change" key="llm.chat.agent.claude.code.clear.sessions.on.byok.token.change" /> </extensions> </idea-plugin>

key="llm.daemon.insights.authorship.threshold.days" defaultValue="183" description="Threshold in days for showing insights on own code - insights will be shown if own code was authored more than this many days ago" restartRequired="false" /> <registryKey key="llm.daemon.insights.authorship.provenance.enable" defaultValue="true" description="Enable provenance-based authorship detection for implementation="com.intellij.ml.llm.daemon.insight.handler.listeners.InsightEditorListener" /> <statistics.counterUsagesCollector
